algorithms.title: AED Operation Sequence
algorithms.description: The universal four-step AED sequence, integrated with ongoing CPR.
steps: Power on the AED as soon as it arrives and follow the voice prompts.
Attach the pads to the patient's bare, dry chest as shown on the pad diagrams (anterolateral or anteroposterior).
Ensure everyone is clear and allow the AED to analyze the rhythm.
If a shock is advised, confirm everyone is clear and press the shock button.
Immediately resume chest compressions after the shock (or after 'no shock advised'), without checking a pulse.
Continue CPR for about 2 minutes until the AED prompts the next analysis; use that pause to switch compressors.
Repeat analyze–shock–CPR cycles until the patient shows signs of life or advanced providers take over.

algorithms.title: AED Special-Situation Adjustments
algorithms.description: Quick fixes for common obstacles to safe, effective pad placement.
steps: Wet chest: move off standing water and wipe the chest dry before applying pads.
Implanted pacemaker/ICD: place the pad at least about 1 inch (2.5 cm) from the device bump.
Medication patch: remove the patch and wipe the skin, then apply the pad.
Hairy chest: press pads firmly; if they will not adhere, quickly shave the area and use fresh pads.
Small child: if pads would touch, use anteroposterior (front/back) placement.
Pregnancy: use the AED normally — defibrillation is safe and indicated.
